summary: Mobile responsive table fix verified end-to-end. On 375x812 iPhone viewport, all page-level data tables in portal (client invoices/order + admin invoices/users/products/crm) correctly engage horizontal scroll (container overflow-x=auto, table scrollWidth>=720px, container clientWidth=341px, container.scrollWidth > clientWidth). Desktop regression at 1440x900 clean — sidebar sticky (position:sticky, top:0px) retained from iteration_7. Sidebar hamburger opens (transform=matrix(1,0,0,1,0,0), translate-x-0) on mobile. Product grid on /portal/client/order stacks to 1 column (grid-template-columns=343px) on mobile as expected.
critical_code_review_comments: Note: /portal/admin/orders and /portal/client/tickets & /portal/client/services use card-based layouts (not <table>), so they do not need — and were not affected by — the min-w-[720px]/overflow-x-auto fix. This is fine; the fix scope covered only actual page-level <table> containers.

Suggestion (non-blocking): Consider a global shared TableContainer component wrapping `rounded-2xl bg-white border border-slate-200 overflow-x-auto` + child <table className='w-full min-w-[720px] text-sm'> to enforce the pattern in future pages and avoid drift.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@intercloud-digital.com / AdminIntercloud2026! · Client: demo@client.com / ClientDemo2026!
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 confirmed the mobile table-scroll CSS layout fix works across 4 admin table pages and 2 client table pages. /portal/admin/orders, /portal/client/tickets and /portal/client/services intentionally use card layouts (no <table>), so they are exempt and returned no table containers — this is expected behavior, not a bug. Desktop layout unaffected. Sidebar sticky-top from iteration_7 still intact.
